entries.title: Epitalon extends telomere length via hTERT upregulation in normal human cells and activates ALT pathway in cancer cells
entries.authors: Ullah H, Zubair M, Beg MA, Khan S, Ahmad S, Sohail A, et al.
entries.keyFindings: First independent Western replication confirming epithalon activates telomerase via hTERT upregulation in normal human cells; discovered ALT pathway activation in cancer cells
entries.limitations: In vitro only; no human clinical translation

entries.title: Epithalon: A Comprehensive 25-Year Review of Geroprotective and Neuroendocrine Effects
entries.authors: Cardinali DP, Golombek DA, et al.
entries.keyFindings: Comprehensive 25-year literature review; confirms geroprotective mechanisms; notes minimal adverse events but lacks rigorous Phase 3 data
entries.limitations: Review of existing literature; no new clinical data

entries.title: Epitalon stimulates telomerase and enhances mitochondrial function in bovine cumulus cells
entries.authors: Ullah H, Ahmad S, Khan S, et al.
entries.keyFindings: Epitalon increases telomerase activity, enhances mitochondrial health, reduces reactive oxygen species
entries.limitations: Animal cell model; translation to humans unknown

sources.section: Original Russian Research - Khavinson Group (St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology)

entries.title: Peptide promotes overcoming of the division limit in human somatic cell
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Bondarev IE, Butyugov AA
entries.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
entries.keyFindings: Epithalon treatment allowed cells to exceed Hayflick limit by 44%; claimed telomerase activation
entries.limitations: Single lab, not independently replicated until 2025

entries.title: Effect of epithalon on the activity of telomerase in human somatic cells
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Sherbakov NV, Ryzhak GA
entries.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
entries.keyFindings: First report of epithalon-induced telomerase activation in human cells
entries.limitations: Foundational study but not independently verified for 24 years

entries.title: Effect of epithalon on the lifespan increase in Drosophila melanogaster
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Izmaylov DM, Obukhova LK, Malinin VV
entries.keyFindings: 11.3% increase in median lifespan; 8% increase in maximum lifespan in fruit flies
entries.limitations: Invertebrate model; limited translation to humans

entries.title: Effect of short peptides on chromatin in lymphocytes in senile subjects
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Lezhava TA, Malinin VV
entries.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
entries.keyFindings: Epithalon treatment reduced heterochromatin granule concentration in lymphocytes; suggested epigenetic modulation
entries.limitations: Small sample, no placebo control, single group

entries.title: Effect of vilon and epithalon on chromatin in lymphocytes from old people
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Lezhava TA
entries.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
entries.keyFindings: Chromatin decondensation observed after peptide treatment; potential epigenetic mechanism
entries.limitations: Uncontrolled observational study

entries.title: The pineal peptides: interaction with indoles and the role in aging and cancer
entries.authors: Anisimov VN, Khavinson VK, Popovich IG, Zabezhinski MA
entries.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of pineal peptide-melatonin interactions; proposed anti-aging mechanisms
entries.limitations: Review based on single-group research

entries.title: Melatonin and epithalamin increase lifespan of female SHR mice
entries.authors: Anisimov VN, Khavinson VK, Popovich IG, Zabezhinski MA, et al.
entries.keyFindings: Epithalamin (epithalon precursor) increased lifespan by 31%; reduced tumor incidence
entries.limitations: Animal model; not replicated

entries.title: Effect of melatonin and pineal peptide preparation epithalamin on life span and free radical oxidation in Drosophila melanogaster
entries.authors: Anisimov VN, Mylnikov SV, Oparina TI, Khavinson VK
entries.keyFindings: Epithalamin reduced lipid peroxidation; modestly increased lifespan in flies
entries.limitations: Early foundational study; invertebrate model

entries.title: Short Peptides Regulate Gene Expression, Telomere Length and Chromatin Structure
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Linkova NS, Tarnovskaya SI
entries.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of AEDG and other short peptides; proposed epigenetic mechanisms for telomere regulation
entries.limitations: Self-citation review; no new experimental data

entries.title: AEDG Peptide (Epithalon) Stimulates Gene Expression and Protein Synthesis during Neurogenesis: Possible Epigenetic Mechanism
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Linkova NS, Polyakova VO, Kheifets OV, Tarnovskaya SI, Kvetnoy IM
entries.keyFindings: Epithalon upregulated neurogenesis-related genes; proposed epigenetic mechanism via DNA methylation changes
entries.limitations: In vitro only; single research group

entries.title: Peptide Geroprotector AEDG Decondenses Heterochromatin in Human Lymphocytes
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Kuznik BI, Tarnovskaya SI, Linkova NS
entries.keyFindings: AEDG treatment reduced heterochromatin content; suggested chromatin remodeling mechanism
entries.limitations: Small sample; no long-term follow-up

entries.title: Peptide bioregulation of aging: results of 35-year investigation
entries.authors: Khavinson VK
entries.keyFindings: Comprehensive 35-year review of peptide bioregulation research; includes epithalon/epithalamin studies
entries.limitations: Russian language; single-group research

entries.title: Effect of epithalon on age-related changes in melatonin concentration in monkeys
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Goncharova ND, Lapin BA
entries.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
entries.keyFindings: Epithalon restored nighttime melatonin levels in aged monkeys to youthful concentrations
entries.limitations: Small primate sample; not independently replicated

entries.title: Effect of epithalon on the age-related changes in rat pineal gland
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Kvetnoy IM, Popuchiev VV, Yuzhakov VV, Kotlova LN
entries.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
entries.keyFindings: Epithalon treatment increased pinealocyte functional activity; restored melatonin synthesis capacity
entries.limitations: Animal model; histological outcomes only

entries.title: The Role of Small Peptides in the Modulation of Aging and Cancer
entries.authors: Fedoreyeva LI, Kireev II, Khavinson VK, Vanyushin BF
entries.keyFindings: Reviews short peptide mechanisms including epithalon; discusses epigenetic regulation of aging genes
entries.limitations: Khavinson co-authored; review of own work

entries.title: Short Peptides and Their Analogs as Biofunctional Agents: Molecular Design Based on Natural Patterns
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Tarnovskaya SI, Linkova NS, Pronyaeva VE
entries.keyFindings: Molecular modeling of short peptide-DNA interactions; proposed binding mechanisms for AEDG
entries.limitations: Computational modeling; requires experimental validation

entries.title: Peptide Regulation of Aging: 35-Year Research Experience
entries.authors: Khavinson VK
entries.keyFindings: Historical perspective on peptide bioregulation research; clinical outcomes summary
entries.limitations: Retrospective review by developer

entries.title: The Effects of Peptide Bioregulators on the Visual Functions in Patients with Retinitis Pigmentosa
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Razumovsky MI, Trofimova SV, Razumovskaya AM, Trofimov AV
entries.keyFindings: Epithalon plus retinalamin showed visual function improvements; small pilot study
entries.limitations: Uncontrolled pilot; combined with other peptide; small sample

entries.title: Epithalamin: a synthetic tetrapeptide with pineal gland extract activity
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Morozov VG
entries.keyFindings: Original characterization of synthetic AEDG peptide derived from bovine pineal gland extract epithalamin
entries.limitations: Early foundational work

entries.title: Detection of AEDG Peptide (Epithalon) in Human Pineal Gland
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Linkova NS, Kvetnoy IM, Kvetnaia TV, Polyakova VO, Korf HW
entries.keyFindings: First detection of endogenous AEDG peptide in human pineal gland tissue
entries.limitations: Post-mortem tissue; functional significance unclear

entries.title: Effect of epithalamin on immune function in elderly patients
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Morozov VG, Anisimov VN
entries.keyFindings: Improved immune markers including T-cell counts after treatment course
entries.limitations: Uncontrolled; Russian language publication; methodological details limited

entries.title: 15-year experience of thymic peptide use for prophylaxis of oncological and other age-related diseases
entries.authors: Khavinson VK, Morozov VG
entries.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
entries.keyFindings: Claimed 2-fold reduction in mortality over 6 years with peptide treatment including epithalon
entries.limitations: Retrospective; combined treatments; no randomization; significant bias risk

criticalAnalysis.singleSourceBias: Approximately 95% of all epithalon research originates from Vladimir Khavinson and colleagues at the St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology
criticalAnalysis.replicationStatus: First independent Western replication achieved in 2025 (Ullah et al., Biogerontology) confirming telomerase activation in vitro
criticalAnalysis.methodologicalConcerns: Historical Russian studies lack rigorous controls, randomization details, blinding, and detailed methodology reporting
criticalAnalysis.peerReviewQuality: Early studies published in translated Russian journals with variable peer review standards; more recent work in indexed international journals
criticalAnalysis.humanTrialGaps: No Phase 2 or Phase 3 controlled human clinical trials; all human data observational or small pilots
criticalAnalysis.positiveUpdate2025: Independent 2025 research provides first external validation of telomerase activation mechanism, though human clinical efficacy remains unproven

developerContext.name: Vladimir Khavinson
developerContext.affiliation: St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology, Russia
developerContext.credentials: Full member of Russian Academy of Medical Sciences; Director of Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology
developerContext.legacy: Pioneer of peptide bioregulation theory; developed multiple bioregulatory peptides including Epithalon, Thymalin, Cortexin

notes: Epithalon research has entered a new phase with 2025 independent Western validation of telomerase activation mechanisms. However, almost all published research still originates from the Khavinson group. The 2025 Ullah et al. study in Biogerontology represents a significant milestone as the first independent replication of core claims. Human clinical efficacy remains completely unproven, with no controlled trials conducted. Evidence strength upgraded from 'very-low' to 'low' for mechanistic claims based on 2025 independent replication, but clinical claims remain 'very-low'.
